title: 1.1: Removes the if ( items.length === 1 ) conditional check from getConfirmText function

annotations.description: Verifies that the getConfirmText function no longer contains the if (items.length === 1) conditional statement that previously branched between single-item and multi-item logic.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 9 shows removal of 'if ( items.length === 1 ) {' and subsequent lines show direct access to items[0] without the conditional.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'There's still some leftover code handling cases that can't happen anymore'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] ActionRenderModal usages show items is always passed as a single-element array. [3: Logical Necessity] Dead code that can't execute should be removed to simplify the logic. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation removes unreachable code paths. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove the items.length === 1 conditional check in getConfirmText.

title: 1.2: Removes the multi-select switch statement that used items.length for pluralization in getConfirmText

annotations.description: Verifies that getConfirmText no longer contains the fallback switch statement that used _n() with items.length to generate messages like 'You are about to activate %d selected plugins.'
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 218-290 show removal of the multi-select fallback switch statement that pluralized based on items.length.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states to 'remove that dead code'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The multi-select code path using items.length is unreachable since ActionRenderModal always receives a single-element items array. [3: Logical Necessity] Unreachable code adds complexity without value. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation eliminates dead code paths. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove the multi-select fallback switch statement.

title: 1.3: Removes the if ( selected.length === 1 ) conditional check from buildSuccessPrefix function

annotations.description: Verifies that the buildSuccessPrefix function no longer contains the if (selected.length === 1) conditional statement that previously branched between single-item and multi-item logic.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 300-342 show removal of the if (selected.length === 1) block, leaving only direct access to selected[0].name.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states to 'simplify how the confirmation texts are built'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The selected parameter always has length 1 since it receives the items array which is always single-element. [3: Logical Necessity] Simplification requires removing unnecessary conditionals. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation removes dead branching logic. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove the selected.length === 1 conditional check in buildSuccessPrefix.

title: 1.4: Removes the multi-select switch statement that used selected.length for pluralization in buildSuccessPrefix

annotations.description: Verifies that buildSuccessPrefix no longer contains the fallback switch statement that used _n() with selected.length to generate messages like 'Activated %d plugins'.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 347-404 show removal of the multi-select pluralization using _n() and selected.length in buildSuccessPrefix.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states to 'remove that dead code' and 'simplify how the confirmation texts are built'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The multi-select code path is unreachable since selected always contains one element. [3: Logical Necessity] Dead code must be removed for simplification. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation cleans up unreachable paths. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove the multi-select fallback in buildSuccessPrefix.

title: 1.7: Returns confirmation messages for all six action types (activate, deactivate, update, enable-autoupdate, disable-autoupdate, delete) in getConfirmText

annotations.description: Verifies that getConfirmText produces distinct confirmation messages when called with each of the six actionId values. The implementation structure (switch, object map, if-else chain, etc.) is not prescribed.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 26-293 show all six action types are handled and return appropriate confirmation messages.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires 'keeping the same user-visible behavior'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original code handles all six action types. [3: Logical Necessity] Behavior preservation requires all action types to remain functional. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation maintains feature parity. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must produce confirmation messages for all six action types.

title: 1.8: Returns all 4 context-specific delete confirmation messages based on the extraActions array in getConfirmText (deactivate+disable-autoupdate combined, deactivate-only, disable-autoupdate-only, default remove-only)

annotations.description: Verifies that when actionId is 'delete', getConfirmText returns different confirmation messages based on the contents of the extraActions array (which may include 'deactivate' and/or 'disable-autoupdate').
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 119-293 show the delete case produces distinct messages based on extraActions array contents.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires 'keeping the same user-visible behavior and copy'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original delete case has conditional messages based on extraActions. [3: Logical Necessity] Context-specific delete messages must be preserved. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation maintains conditional message logic. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must preserve context-specific delete confirmation messages based on extraActions.

title: 1.9: Returns a fallback message for unrecognized actionId values in getConfirmText

annotations.description: Verifies that getConfirmText returns a generic fallback message (such as 'You are about to perform this action.') when called with an actionId that does not match any of the six known action types.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 294-295 shows a default fallback returning __('You are about to perform this action.').
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires 'keeping the same user-visible behavior'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original code has a default fallback case. [3: Logical Necessity] Edge case handling must be preserved. [4: Quality Necessity] Any good implementation handles unexpected inputs gracefully. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must handle unknown actionId values with a fallback message.

title: 4.1: Alters the string 'You are about to activate the %1$s plugin on %2$d site' in getConfirmText

annotations.description: Detects if the activate confirmation message string 'You are about to activate the %1$s plugin on %2$d site.' or its plural variant has been modified or removed from getConfirmText.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch shows the message string 'You are about to activate the %1$s plugin on %2$d site.' is preserved exactly.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states 'keeping the same user-visible behavior and copy'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The existing strings are already translated. [3: Logical Necessity] Changing translated strings would break localization. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must preserve the exact message strings.
